Empires & Puzzles Christine Daae Review: The Operatic Assassin

She sings, she shreds, and she accidentally cleanses your enemies if you aren't careful. Discover why Christine Daae is the most technical fast hitter in the game and if she's worth your emblems in 2026.
Christine Daae Review: Is This Phantom Star Still Operatic Gold?
Christine Daae, The Phantom's Muse: Singing Your Enemies to Sleep (and Your Debuffs Away)
Christine Daae, the star soprano of the

family, is a Fast mana Holy hero who brings a unique mix of aggression and control to the battlefield. Released in late 2023, she was designed as a "Hit-3" damage dealer with a twist: her songs don't just hurt; they force enemies to dance, disabling them in unique ways. While she lacks the raw stat horsepower of the 2026 gods walking among us, her kit offers a specific utility—combining a dispel, a cleanse (for the enemy, strangely), and a hefty debuff cocktail—that keeps her relevant for tacticians who know how to conduct her properly.

Christine is depicted in her iconic white gown, mid-performance, radiating the elegance and drama of the stage. The art captures her role as the innocent yet powerful muse, contrasting sharply with the grim, battle-hardened warriors usually populating your roster. It’s a refreshing change of pace—less "blood and guts," more "arias and applause." She looks like she’s about to hit a high C that shatters your phone screen.
When looking at Christine's stats in the harsh light of 2026, the age lines are visible.
This is significantly lower than modern hitters who regularly base at 1300+. Even with Limit Breaks, she struggles to match the raw output of newer snipers.
Her bulk is now considered "squishy." Modern support heroes often have higher base stats than her maxed values.
She relies heavily on emblems and Limit Breaks to survive. She is no longer a stat-stick; she is a utility piece.
is one of the most mechanically complex "simple" skills in the game.
355% damage to three targets at Fast speed is respectable, though not earth-shattering anymore.
"All existing status effects will be removed when this effect is added."
It dispels the enemy's buffs (Taunt, Counterattack, etc.) AND cleanses their ailments (Defense Down, Burn, etc.).
Applies -40% Defense and -40% Accuracy, plus immunity to new buffs/ailments.
fire her first. If you fire your defense down hero and then Christine, she will wipe your own defense down and replace it with hers. She is a "primer," not a finisher.
Let's break down the soprano's performance:
Fast. She charges in 8 tiles, or 7 with a level 23 Mana Troop (or equivalent bonuses). This speed is her saving grace, allowing her to disrupt enemies before they set up.
Comparison: Modern "Hit-3" heroes often output 5,000+ raw damage. She won't wipe teams alone.

The combination of -40% Defense and -40% Accuracy essentially creates a massive HP swing. Enemies take more damage and miss their return fire, effectively increasing your team's survivability by ~40% against Special Skills.
Family Bonus, Passives, Aether Power & Unique Abilities
The Opera family is all about rhythm and resilience.
The stats boost (Attack/Defense/HP) is standard but welcome, helping patch up her aging stat line if you run her with the Phantom or Madame Giry.
A fantastic passive for a hitter. She ignores Attack Down effects, ensuring her 355% hit always hits with full force.
Gaining 5% health when an ailment expires or is cleansed provides decent sustain, especially in war formats with lots of status effects flying around.
While not the most exciting power for a sniper, it punishes enemies who target her fast-charging mana bar. It adds a layer of "don't touch me" to her squishy profile.
Upon release, Christine was a top-tier flank. Her ability to dismantle Taunt heroes (like Ludwig) and apply heavy defense down at Fast speed made her a staple in high-level wars.

The damage numbers have fallen behind. 355% isn't the execution sentence it used to be. Additionally, the "Dance" mechanic can be blocked by newer heroes with "Immunity to Status Ailments," rendering her utility moot.

. She is no longer a universal defense hero. However, on offense, she remains a potent counter to buff-heavy teams. She is a surgical tool: bring her to strip a Ludwig or a Miriam & Midnight, drop their defense, and let a modern heavy hitter finish the job.
Christine needs to hit hard to justify her spot, but she also needs to live long enough to sing.
Maximizes the 355% hit. With her low base attack, she needs every point to penetrate modern high-defense heroes.
Ensures she survives to cast her debuffs (Defense/Accuracy down), which is her main utility now.
Her damage becomes negligible, turning her into a pure support hero.
path is recommended. If you are bringing a Fast Hit-3 hero, you want them to contribute to the kill. Her debuffs are great, but dead enemies deal zero damage.
Dispels buffs, cleanses (enemies), applies Defense Down, and applies Blind all in one move.
Her "remove effects" mechanic bypasses standard dispel checks if the enemy isn't immune to ailments, making her a soft counter to Taunt.
Fast mana allows her to set the tempo of the match.
Passive resistance to Attack Down means her damage is consistent.
ailments from enemies is a major anti-synergy with DoT heroes or other debuffers if mis-timed.
She is squishy and hits softer than modern peers.
If an enemy has "Immunity to Status Ailments" (e.g., from a Cleric proc, a passive, or a buff like Hathor), her entire dance effect—including the dispel part—can be blocked.
Her best home. Use her to snipe buff-heavy defenses.
She is excellent here, removing enemy buffs and shutting down their damage.
-40% Defense Down and high tile damage (with emblems) make her a solid choice, provided you don't overwrite a stronger Defense Down (like a -56% or -60%).
Christine is a "Setup" hero. She prepares the enemy team to die.
Christine fires first: Strips buffs, applies Def Down.
Erlang Shen fires second: Hits massively hard on the softened targets.
Anne cleans up whatever dust is left.
Pair her with purple or yellow heavy hitters. When Ludwig taunts, Christine fires, removing the Taunt and exposing the neighbors to follow-up attacks.
DoT heroes (Rayne, Gravrave) or heavy ailment stackers (cMarjana)
you can guarantee they fire AFTER her.
(Hathor, Aramis, Toons). If they are immune to the "Dance," the "Remove Effects" clause does not trigger, and she becomes just a weak hitter.

If you pulled her, rejoice! She is a Swiss Army knife. Level her immediately. She will teach you the importance of timing your specials. Don't worry about the stats yet; her utility will carry you through map stages and Platinum raids easily.

She is a luxury tool. If you have the latest 2026 OP heroes, she might sit on the bench. However, for War depth, she is invaluable. She saves you a Dispeller slot and a Defense Down slot. Is she worth an Alpha Aether? Probably not, unless you lack a modern fast yellow hitter. Save the Alphas for heroes with stats that start with a "13".
Originally, Christine hit harder but had weaker debuffs. The community was split when she went live with -40% debuffs but less damage. One user noted,
"Her doing 355% at fast vs. 405 at average for the Phantom is a slap into his face."
Players were confused initially about her interaction with ailment immunity.
"I think this is a bug and should override Aramis’ skill,"
one player argued. (Spoiler: It wasn't a bug; immunity blocks the dance).
The community quickly identified her best friend.
became the consensus for maximizing her potential.

Christine Daae is a hero who aged gracefully into a support role. She may not hit the high notes of damage she once promised, but her ability to strip a team naked (figuratively) and leave them vulnerable (-40% Defense) is timeless. She requires finesse—fire her first, or don't fire her at all—but in the right hands, she conducts a masterpiece of destruction.
A technical marvel that rewards smart play over brute force.

A comprehensive review of Christine Daae from the Secrets of the Opera event. We analyze her fast mana speed, unique Ballad of Passion mechanics, and whether her 2023 stats can still compete in the current meta.
